title: Urban heat islands and green infrastructure
abstract: Green roofs can reduce urban temperatures by 2-5°C through evapotranspiration and increased albedo. This study examines implementation in 10 major cities.

title: Cooling center effectiveness during heat waves
abstract: Community cooling centers reduced heat-related mortality by 40% during the 2022 heat wave. We analyze usage patterns and accessibility.

title: Climate adaptation strategies in low-income neighborhoods
abstract: This systematic review examines 50 studies on heat adaptation interventions. Results show mixed effectiveness depending on community engagement.

title: Photovoltaic panels and building energy efficiency
abstract: Solar PV installations reduced building cooling loads by 15% while generating electricity. Dual benefit for urban heat mitigation.

title: Heat-related hospital admissions in elderly populations
abstract: Emergency department visits increased 3-fold during extreme heat events. Age over 65 was the strongest predictor of admission.
authors: Liu Y, Brown S, Davis P

title: Green roof biodiversity and ecosystem services
abstract: Green roofs support 23 species of native plants and insects while providing cooling benefits. Multi-functional urban infrastructure.

title: Urban planning for climate resilience
abstract: Municipal heat action plans in 15 US cities show common elements: early warning systems, cooling centers, and vulnerable population outreach.

title: Reflective roof coatings and energy savings
abstract: White roof coatings reduced summer cooling costs by 25% in commercial buildings. Low-cost intervention with rapid payback.

title: Social determinants of heat vulnerability
abstract: Income, housing quality, and access to air conditioning explain 70% of variance in heat-related health outcomes across census tracts.

title: Tree canopy coverage and urban temperatures
abstract: Each 10% increase in tree cover associated with 0.5°C temperature reduction. Urban forestry as climate adaptation strategy.
